Open for editing, closed for reading

questions.correct: Open for extension, closed for modification

questions.question: Which device type is used to access the web the most?

Designing for mobile first, then enhancing for desktop

questions.correct: You start with constraints and add features for larger screens

questions.correct: The server doesn't store session info; the JWT itself contains all needed data

questions.question: Which tool helps identify vulnerable dependencies in your project?

Testing where the tester has no knowledge of internal code

questions.question: What is 'Code Smell'?
options: A syntax error that crashes the app
A sign in the code that hints at a deeper design problem
A virus found in the codebase
questions.correct: A sign in the code that hints at a deeper design problem

questions.question: Which is a common pitfall in testing?
options: Only testing the happy path and ignoring edge cases

Making products usable by as many people as possible regardless of ability

questions.question: What is 'MFA'?

Maximum Firewall Activation — a security protocol

questions.question: In cross-platform development, what is a 'Native Module'?

A CSS file bundled with the app

questions.question: Which type of conflict involves disagreements about work content and goals?

Members leave the group one by one
questions.correct: Conflict, power struggles, and disagreements as members assert opinions

questions.question: Which Thomas-Kilmann conflict mode is "High Assertiveness" and "Low Cooperativeness"?

options: Low assertiveness, high cooperativeness — you give in to others
High assertiveness, high cooperativeness — working together for a win-win solution
Medium assertiveness, medium cooperativeness — splitting the difference
Low assertiveness, low cooperativeness — sidestepping the conflict
questions.correct: High assertiveness, high cooperativeness — working together for a win-win solution

questions.question: Which document outlines communication protocols and role assignments for a team?

options: High assertiveness, low cooperativeness — you win, they lose
Low assertiveness, high cooperativeness — you give in fully
Medium assertiveness, medium cooperativeness — both sides give up something
Low assertiveness, low cooperativeness — avoiding the issue
questions.correct: Medium assertiveness, medium cooperativeness — both sides give up something

options: Form a hypothesis about the cause

A breakpoint that pauses code only when a specified condition is true

questions.correct: A breakpoint that pauses code only when a specified condition is true

questions.question: What does 'Structured Logging' mean in the context of backend debugging?

Using only console.error() for all messages
Writing logs to a MongoDB collection
questions.correct: Logging objects in a consistent, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON) with fields like timestamp, route, and userId

options: Always fetches from network, never caches
Deletes old cache on every request
Refuses to load if network is unavailable
Serves cached content immediately while updating the cache in the background
questions.correct: Serves cached content immediately while updating the cache in the background

questions.question: Which is a known limitation of PWAs compared to native apps?
options: Less OS integration and limited access to all device sensors/APIs
questions.correct: Less OS integration and limited access to all device sensors/APIs

questions.question: In the MERN cross-platform architecture, which layer is shared across all frontends?
What command is used to start a new Expo React Native project?
Which of the following is a cross-platform pitfall mentioned in the slides?
What is the recommended best practice for sharing code across platforms in MERN?
options: Share UI components, not backend logic
Never share any code between platforms
Share backend logic, write platform-specific UIs
questions.correct: Share backend logic, write platform-specific UIs

questions.question: React Native's hot reload feature allows developers to:
options: Push app updates to the App Store instantly
Deploy backend changes without restarting Node.js
See UI changes immediately without a full app restart
questions.correct: See UI changes immediately without a full app restart

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the MERN cross-platform architecture?
options: Each platform uses a completely separate backend
Flutter is used as the single frontend for all platforms
Node.js serves all frontends; React web + React Native mobile + Electron desktop share the same API
